title: Defining the Loss & Optimizer
concept: Setting up Cross-Entropy loss and choosing an optimizer (like Adam) with a learning rate schedule.

exercises.prompt: What is the main purpose of a loss function in training a model?

options.text: To measure how well the model is performing
To generate images for the model

exercises.explain: The loss function helps us understand how far off our model's predictions are from the actual results.

cards.title: Understanding Loss and Optimizers
cards.body: In machine learning, loss functions and optimizers work together to help models learn from data. The loss function tells us how wrong our predictions are, while the optimizer updates the model to improve performance.

cards.title: What is Cross-Entropy Loss?
cards.body: Cross-Entropy Loss measures the difference between two probability distributions. It's commonly used in classification tasks to evaluate how well the predicted probabilities match the actual labels.

cards.title: Fishing Analogy
cards.body: Think of loss as a fishing net: the more holes in your net (errors in predictions), the less fish (accuracy) you catch. Cross-Entropy helps us count those holes and fix them.

cards.title: Visual Example of Cross-Entropy
cards.body: If a model predicts a cat with 80% confidence and it's actually a dog, the Cross-Entropy calculates how much the model 'missed' compared to the true label.

cards.title: Myth: All Loss Functions Are the Same
cards.body: Not true! Different tasks require different loss functions. Cross-Entropy is great for classification, but not suitable for regression tasks.

cards.title: Loss vs. Accuracy
cards.body: As training progresses, we ideally want our loss to decrease while accuracy increases.

exercises.prompt: What is a common optimizer used in training models?
exercises.explain: Adam is a popular optimizer as it adjusts the learning rate based on the average of recent gradients, helping the model converge more effectively.

exercises.prompt: The optimizer does not affect the learning rate during training.
exercises.explain: The optimizer is crucial for adjusting the learning rate, which helps in efficiently reaching a solution during training.

exercises.prompt: Cross-Entropy Loss helps measure the difference between predicted probabilities and _____ labels.
exercises.sentence: Cross-Entropy Loss helps measure the difference between predicted probabilities and actual labels.
exercises.explain: Actual labels are the true classifications we want our model to learn.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about learning rates is correct?

options.text: Learning rates can depend on the optimizer

exercises.explain: Learning rates can change based on the optimizer used, as some optimizers support dynamic adjustments throughout training.

exercises.prompt: Why is Cross-Entropy Loss preferred for classification tasks over other loss functions?

options.text: It calculates the average distance between true and predicted values.
It predicts the next value in a sequence.
It evaluates how well predicted probability distributions match the actual distributions.
It works better with unstructured data.

exercises.explain: Cross-Entropy Loss evaluates how well the predicted probabilities align with the actual labels, making it ideal for classification tasks.
